frase_original: I have no idea why, but I'm grinning back at her. This temperamental, wild creature in front of me who has the nerve to put me in my place.

texto: Know what’s worse than cold turkey? Just a little bump. One tiny sip to take the edge off. The edges never went away, they only got sharper. Every addict would tell you. Gray areas couldn’t exist in a sober environment.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de A. Zavarelli que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Know what’s worse than cold turkey? Just a little bump. One tiny sip to take the edge off. The ed…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, addiction, alpha, com foco em reflexao-noturna e alpha.

texto: I needed to channel the darkness that ran through my veins and embrace it. To play this game better than this man ever could have predicted. After all, I’d only been with him twice, and already I could see his weakness. His weakness was me.

texto: Was it cruel if someone asked for it? Begged for it, even? She always begged. Even now, I could hear her whimpering for me. Christ, those noises she made. A one-way ticket to heaven.

frase_original: Was it cruel if someone asked for it? Begged for it, even? She always begged. Even now, I could hear her whimpering for me. Christ, those noises she made. A one-way ticket to heaven.

texto: I didn’t know what to say. What to do. I didn’t feel strong anymore. I felt like I couldn’t go on without him. He loved me fiercely. Obsessively. Rivalled only by the intensity which I felt for him. So why couldn’t we be together?

I didn’t feel strong anymore. I felt like I couldn’t go on…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, alpha, dark-romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e dark-romance.
